extra.reasoning:
The property is VIOLATED. The witness_assignment is the BEFORE state (severe_headache=false); flipping severe_headache to true lowers acuity from emergent to self_care.

severe_headache appears in exactly one rule: interaction_drop (priority 100), which requires the conjunction of all 12 symptoms (sweating, palpitations, vision_loss, rash, chest_pain, fever, vomiting, back_pain, confusion, stiff_neck, focal_weakness, severe_headache) and sets acuity := self_care (the LOWEST acuity). No rule raises acuity from severe_headache.

BEFORE (severe_headache=false): interaction_drop does not fire because its severe_headache conjunct is false. The firing rules include raise_rash, raise_palpitations, raise_stiff_neck, raise_fever, raise_back_pain — all priority 3, all setting acuity := emergent. The highest-priority firing rule is priority 3, so acuity = emergent.

AFTER (severe_headache=true, all else unchanged): now all 12 conjuncts are true, so interaction_drop (priority 100) fires and wins over every priority-3 rule, setting acuity := self_care.

Transition emergent (highest) -> self_care (lowest) is a strict decrease, directly violating the monotonicity requirement that setting severe_headache true never lowers acuity. dyspnea is set false to keep the example minimal (its value is irrelevant since dyspnea's emergent rule, even if firing, is still beaten by priority 100). This is a classic priority-inversion / interaction-rule trap: the very-high-priority "drop" rule overrides all the protective emergent rules precisely when the extra symptom completes the conjunction.
